description: Brands commit a fixed amount from each product sale to charitable causes, positioning purchases as direct contributions to social good while building brand purpose.
structural_recipe: Post product/menu item with explicit donation commitment (e.g. 'X riyal from each purchase goes to charity'). Tag the charitable organization or campaign. Use cause-aligned hashtags and emoji (💚 for charity). Frame as customer participation in goodwill, not brand charity. Include visual of product or donation impact. Time around religious/charitable seasons (Ramadan).
why_it_works: Saudi consumers strongly value zakat and charitable giving as religious obligation and social responsibility. Embedding charity into purchase decisions transforms consumption into moral action, reducing purchase guilt and aligning customer values with brand values. Creates positive social proof and differentiates commodity products through purpose.
transplantation_caveats: Donation must be genuine and verifiable—false claims violate Islamic ethics and Saudi consumer trust
Partner organizations should be legitimate and aligned with Islamic principles
Avoid performative charity; transparency on donation amounts and recipient impact is critical
Cannot be used during non-charitable seasons without seeming opportunistic
cultural_constraints: Charity must align with Islamic zakat principles and Saudi social values
Transparency and authenticity are non-negotiable in Saudi market
Must avoid transactional framing of charity—emphasis on shared moral purpose
